Missing Report, in this connection, was also filed on 12.03.2010 at 
Police Station Udaipur, District Pratapgarh, Uttar Pradesh.
11. Reliance was placed on the decisions in Bir Singh (supra), Rajesh 
Jain (supra) and Dattatraya v. Sharanappa, 2024 SCC OnLine SC 
1899, to highlight that the appellant did not discharge his burden 
of establishing the factual basis to activate the presumptive clause. 
It was further submitted, that in any case, the complaint is not 
maintainable since the drawer of the cheque i.e., the Partnership 
Firm viz. M/s Sun Enterprises, has not been arrayed as a party. 
Additionally, learned senior counsel also placed reliance on the 
decisions in John K John v. Tom Varghese, (2007) 12 SCC 714; 
Krishna Janardhan Bhat v. Dattatraya G Hegde, (2008) 4 SCC 
54, and; G Pankajakshi Amma v. Mathai Mathew (Dead) through 
LRs., (2004) 12 SCC 83. 
12. While it was urged that the appeal be dismissed, without prejudice
